mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage, raising expected earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest vintage for 2010-06 was 92.5354 and available before 2010-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 forward returns than lower INDPRO growth in walk-forward tests, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals renewed labor-market deterioration, lower household income growth, and weaker earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over the prior 3 monthly observations
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest vintage for 2010-07 was 9.5 and available before 2010-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have positive 20-day S&P 500 forward returns more than 60% of the time or if their mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales and payroll employment both rise over three months, stronger consumption demand is confirmed by income support, increasing revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest vintage for 2010-07 was 362688 and PAYEMS latest vintage for 2010-07 was 130242
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS and PAYEMS condition does not outperform cases where only one series is positive, or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in walk-forward return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25% and CPI inflation is not accelerating, discount-rate pressure is minimal and liquidity support dominates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change is below its prior 12-month change
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest vintage for 2010-07 was 0.18 and CPIAUCSL latest vintage for 2010-07 was 217.597
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations fail to produce above-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal is negative in more than half of rolling 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 600 thousand with negative 3-month momentum signals weak residential investment and impaired household balance-sheet repair, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest vintage for 2010-06 was 549 and available before 2010-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances have mean 60-day S&P 500 forward returns equal to or above the unconditional mean, or if downside hit rate is below 45% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: If CPI inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, rising input costs are not offset by policy normalization, compressing real purchasing power and margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.5 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds its prior 12-month change by > 1pp
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI acceleration and low FEDFUNDS interaction has a positive return coefficient, or if triggered 20-day returns are not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Positive payroll growth while unemployment remains above 8% signals an early-cycle labor rebound with large slack, allowing earnings recovery without immediate wage inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest vintage for 2010-07 was 130242 and UNRATE latest vintage for 2010-07 was 9.5
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not outperform periods with negative PAYEMS growth and UNRATE > 8, or if 60-day forward return hit rate is below the unconditional positive rate.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals immediate weakness in household spending and revenue expectations, causing short-horizon equity repricing and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest vintage for 2010-07 was 362688 and available before 2010-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if negative RSAFS monthly changes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional positive rate, or if mean triggered return is not below zero.
